violations.finding: Direct python3 invocation without venv activation: 'echo "$stdin_json" | python3 "$HOOK" >/dev/null 2>"$WORK/stderr.log"'
violations.recommendation: Activate venv before invoking python hooks: 'source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && echo ... | python "$HOOK"'

violations.finding: Direct python3 invocation without venv activation: 'echo "{...}" | python3 "$HOOK" >/dev/null 2>"$STDERR_A_LOG"'
violations.recommendation: Activate venv before invoking python hooks: 'source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && echo ... | python "$HOOK"'

violations.finding: Direct python3 invocation without venv activation: 'python3 - <<PYEOF' inline heredoc execution
violations.recommendation: Extract inline python to a named script and invoke via venv: 'source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python scripts/generate-test-transcript.py'

violations.finding: Hardcoded path literal '/root/.claude/docs/dev/' with no parameter override; CONTROL_ROOT variable exists but is not applied to the .claude-prefixed candidate
violations.recommendation: Replace hardcoded candidate with: "${CLAUDE_ROOT:-/root/.claude}/docs/dev/close-report-${TASK_ID}.md" where CLAUDE_ROOT is an added configurable parameter

violations.finding: HOOK variable hardcoded to '/root/.claude/hooks/stop-spec-coverage-enforce.py' with no parameter fallback
violations.recommendation: Use: HOOK="${HOOK:-/root/.claude/hooks/stop-spec-coverage-enforce.py}" to allow override in different environments

violations.finding: Same as no-hardcoded-values finding: hardcoded /root/.claude/docs/dev/ path has no environment variable or CLI parameter override
violations.recommendation: Add CLAUDE_ROOT parameter with default: CLAUDE_ROOT="${CLAUDE_ROOT:-/root/.claude}" and reference it for the third candidate path

violations.finding: HOOK path not parameterized; test harness cannot be pointed at an alternative hook installation without editing the file

Contains CJK characters '行动 (act), not just 读 (read)' in prose explanation of 'Why four fields?'. These are NOT mascot rank labels (exempt list: 见习学徒, 初级工匠, 熟练工匠, 资深工匠, 宗师级 only) and have no ticket attribution in docs/dev/ticket-*.md.
violations.recommendation: Replace with English-only prose, e.g.: 'The free-text action proves behavioral follow-through, not merely reading.'

violations.finding: Contains CJK characters '行动 (act), not just 读 (read)' in prose explanation of 'Why four fields?'. Same non-exempt CJK boilerplate as agents/ba.md:777.
violations.recommendation: Replace with English-only prose, e.g.: 'The free-text action proves behavioral follow-through, not merely reading.'

violations.finding: Contains CJK characters '行动 (act), not just 读 (read)' in prose explanation of 'Why four fields?'. Same non-exempt CJK boilerplate as agents/ba.md:777.
violations.recommendation: Replace with English-only prose, e.g.: 'The free-text action proves behavioral follow-through, not merely reading.'

violations.finding: Contains verbatim non-English user quote '自我总结的内容质量靠 orchestrator 即兴生成：无所谓' inline in command file. Verbatim user-binding quotes must be stored in docs/dev/ticket-*.md; command files must use English with ticket attribution.
violations.recommendation: Replace the parenthetical Chinese text with English attribution: 'Original user wording preserved in docs/dev/ticket-20260524-205206.md under R5.'

standards_passed: no-inline-code (Standard 1) — N/A for hooks/lib/README.md and hooks/tests/README.md (auto-generated index files, not command/agent prompts); no command/agent .md files in changed-files set
integer-step-numbering (Standard 4) — no decimal or lettered step numbering found in any changed file
meaningful-naming (Standard 5) — no forbidden naming patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, *-v2, etc.) found

english-only (Standard 6 — changed files only) — no CJK/Chinese characters found in the 10 changed files themselves; Standard 6 unconditional scan of agents/* and commands/* produced 4 pre-existing violations listed above (agents/ba.md:777, agents/qa.md:1297, agents/dev.md:497, commands/close.md:435); style-inspector.md:333 mascot rank labels are exempt per spec-20260518-225715 §5.1
merge-similar-scripts (Standard 7) — N/A for hooks/lib/README.md and hooks/tests/README.md (not scripts); no duplicate functionality among changed scripts
concise-documentation (Standard 8) — N/A for hooks/lib/README.md and hooks/tests/README.md (auto-generated index files, not command/agent prompts); no .md command/agent files in the changed set

dangling-references (Standard 10) — all files referenced in hooks/lib/README.md (allowlist.py, bash_context_strip.py, subagent.py, todo_canonical.py) and hooks/tests/README.md (test_bash_safety_context.py, test_cp_checkin.py) verified to exist on disk; hooks/stop-spec-coverage-enforce.py referenced at tests/verify-stop-spec-session-isolation.sh:9 also exists
merge-conflicts (Standard 11) — no conflict markers (<<<<<<</=======/>>>>>>>) found in any of the 10 changed files

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed 5 issues: (1) Findings #8-10 (行动/读 in ba.md, qa.md, dev.md) are genuine Standard 6 violations, not covered by mascot-label exemption — prose translations are not the five exempt rank labels; (2) Finding #11 (close.md Chinese quote) is genuine — verbatim user-binding quotes must be in docs/dev/ticket-*.md, not inline in command files; (3) Findings #1-3 (python3 in tests/*.sh) are correctly classified; (4) Findings #4-7 (hardcoded paths) are not false positives; (5) README all-pass wording for Standards 1/7/8/9 should say N/A rather than 'passed' since those standards do not apply to auto-generated index files. One OBSERVATION_ONLY: no missed Standard 7 or 9 violations in README files.

findings.issue: Findings #8-10 (行动/读 in ba.md:777, qa.md:1297, dev.md:497): genuine Standard 6 violations; prose translations are NOT covered by mascot rank label exemption (exempt list is 见习学徒, 初级工匠, 熟练工匠, 资深工匠, 宗师级 only)
findings.proposed_fix: Replace each sentence with: 'The free-text action proves behavioral follow-through, not merely reading.'
findings.rationale: Confirmed genuine violations. Added findings #8-10 to the violations array.

findings.issue: Finding #11 (commands/close.md:435): verbatim non-English user quote must be in docs/dev/ticket-*.md only; command file must use English with ticket attribution
findings.proposed_fix: Replace the parenthetical with: 'Original user wording preserved in docs/dev/ticket-20260524-205206.md under R5.'
findings.rationale: Confirmed genuine violation. Added as finding #11.

findings.issue: Findings #1-3 (python3 in tests/*.sh): correctly classified; tests/*.sh is in Standard 3 scope; bare python3 calls are violations even in test harnesses
findings.proposed_fix: Activate venv once near top; set PYTHON_BIN="${PYTHON_BIN:-python}"; replace bare python3 calls with $PYTHON_BIN.
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed no false positives; recommendation text updated in findings #1-3.

findings.issue: Findings #4-7 (hardcoded /root/.claude/... paths): confirmed not false positives
findings.proposed_fix: Use CLAUDE_ROOT="${CLAUDE_ROOT:-${CONTROL_ROOT:-/root}/.claude}" and derive paths from it.
findings.rationale: Confirmed genuine violations; recommendation text updated in findings #4-7.

findings.issue: README all-pass wording for Standards 1/7/8/9 overclaims 'passed'; should say N/A for auto-generated index files not in command/agent/script scope
findings.proposed_fix: Report 'N/A: not command/agent/script scope' for Standards 1, 7, 8, 9 on README files.
findings.rationale: standards_passed descriptions updated to clearly say N/A where applicable.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: No missed Standard 7 or Standard 9 violations in hooks/lib/README.md or hooks/tests/README.md; all referenced files verified to exist
findings.rationale: Confirmation that README all-pass assessment is correct (after N/A wording fix).

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied all 5 blocker/major findings: (1-2) added Standard 6 violations for ba.md:777, qa.md:1297, dev.md:497, close.md:435; (3) confirmed Standard 3 findings are correct, updated recommendation text; (4) confirmed Standard 2/9 hardcoded-path findings are genuine, updated recommendation text; (5) corrected standards_passed wording for README files to say N/A where Standards 1/7/8/9 do not apply.
